published_prior_art.cite: SoferRichardsonColicinoSchwartzTchetgen2016 and MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024 cover NOC-DiD and double-negative-control bridges, but I found no paired finite pretrend-indistinguishability certificate in them; sources: https://scholars.mssm.edu/en/publications/on-negative-outcome-control-of-unobserved-confounding-as-a-genera-2/ and https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/24754269.2024.2390748

MiaoGengTchetgen2018 and MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024 already give proxy/rank bridge identification logic; this is a DiD-preperiod specialization. Sources: https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/105/4/987/5073056 and https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/24754269.2024.2390748
RambachanRoth2023 studies robust inference under parallel-trends violations, not exposure-proxy bridge diagnostics; source: https://colab.ws/articles/10.1093/restud/rdad018

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 defines the focal certificate using m(P^a) != m(P^b), but the same exhibit makes B(P^b) singular, so m(P^b) is undefined and the certificate is not actually computed as defined.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The impossibility target names pretrend/placebo/lead diagnostics, but no cited paper is quoted as claiming an NCO-only scalar is an iff diagnostic for a proximal bridge; RambachanRoth2023 is a sensitivity-analysis comparator, not that bridge-frontier claim.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Section 6 defines m(P) only when B(P) is nonsingular, but Conjecture 1 and Exhibit 9.1 compare m(P^a) to m(P^b) while B(P^b) has determinant zero.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed open-neighborhood persistence cannot be supported by the displayed P^b witness, because BR failure is caused by det B(P^b)=0, a rank-deficient equality stratum rather than an open strict inequality.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: Given the Conjecture 1 pair, the no-scalar conclusion follows by the equal-input argument T(P)=phi(S_W(P)); the proof route has fewer than three substantive steps beyond definitions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace P^b with a nonsingular-bridge law where m(P^b) is defined and differs from m(P^a), while BR fails by a nonzero bridge residual rather than determinant zero.
Rewrite the openness claim around strict residual inequalities and nonzero determinants; do not use a singular rank-failure witness for an open-class flagship claim.
For Conjecture 3, name a specific published diagnostic or estimator claim being refuted, with section/equation anchor, or downgrade it to a corollary of Conjecture 1.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version; the drafter should pivot or rebuild the certificate with both bridge loadings defined from full positive paired laws before resubmission.
